title: How to use the Back Office to manage customer requests

topics.topic_name: CCBM - Customer Care By Back Market

bodyHtml:
Starting October 6th., all your customer requests will be managed directly by our customer support team (Customer Care by Back Market - more information here ) and the design of the after-sales platform will therefore be adapted. Requests will only be visible to you whenever your intervention is needed, except customer messages which will not be displayed. However, you can still search for all requests by order number.

The feature is already live since middle of September for sellers using Back Care.
 


 

Back to basics

When going to the "Customer Care" tab, you will still have the four main topics available, as usual:
  • Open tickets
  • Product returns
  • Closed
  • Invoices
The most important thing to keep in mind is that Back Market is taking over all after-sales requests and that you will no longer intervene directly with the customer.
 

What we are expecting from you

You will be able to see all automatic messages and those we consider important for you. However, the request will always be managed by our Care agents, mainly without your intervention. If your help is needed, the information will be displayed to you, so that you easily know what to do. Please note that this page will continue to change in the coming weeks, as we're currently developing a new project (3-click resolution) that will be deployed by end of the year and allow you to manage those requests even faster.


Let's go through the different tabs!
 

1. Open requests


This tab is the most important for you! When Back Market agents send you messages asking for your assistance, you’ll receive an email notification and the customer request will show in this tab.

A customer request in “Open tickets” signals to you that your intervention is necessary and that you need to provide a response within 24 hours. Here you will see all customer requests, but without having all details.

We're displaying the most important information to you, the rest will be managed by our customer care agents.
Therefore, when an agent sends an informative message, the customer request won’t show up in “Open tickets,” and you won’t receive an email because no action is required from you.
 


How to see photos and attachments?
 

Our customer care agents will have the possibility to share photos and attachments with you. You will be able to see those shared elements in the "Details" section, just on the top of a request. Here, you will also find all customer details and related information.

 

I do not agree with a decision
 

Same than before October 6th., you will not be able to complain every taken decision. However, if your complaint concerns a situation stated in the Charter (example: contestation of a warranty exclusion, after the customer have received a solution), you can reach out to the Seller Support Center. Please note that the process is not changing and that the Seller Support Team will therefore not receive any request that is not linked to a justified complaint.


 

2. Product returns

This tab is also crucial for you because it displays all the items that have been returned to your location and require action. Each ticket in this tab will show the total amount of time the returned item has been at your returned location. 
 

 

3. Closed

This tab shows all customer requests that are closed or pending. Back Market agents will ensure that customers are answered and will escalate requests to you if necessary, so there’s no need to monitor this tab.

 

4. Invoices

This tab shows all customer invoice requests. You can upload invoices directly here. Make sure to verify this tab regularly, to avoid having customers waiting for too long.

 

Additional information
 

We recommend that you check the “Open tickets” and “Item returns” tabs once per business day to understand what’s required of you to resolve customer requests. 
 

Within a customer request, you’ll see the following: 
 

  • An information bar displaying the customer’s request and issue, along with the return label if applicable.

  • Messages from Back Market agents: All Back Market agents have been trained on when to escalate customer requests to you, and what information’s required from you to process the customer’s request (note that messages between the Back Market agent and customer will not be shown within the request — you’ll only see necessary information to save time) 

  • Automatic messages sent in the customer request: Most commonly, automatic messages show up when a return is on its way, when a return is delivered to you, and when a refund has been issued.

  • Details about the order and customer, including all attachments sent by the customer (e.g., photos of their item).

  • Various buttons for sending messages to agents, refunding orders, and closing tickets.
